mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a scheduling criterion?
mcqs.explanation: Memory size is not a scheduling criterion. The main scheduling criteria are CPU utilization, throughput, turnaround time, waiting time, and response time.

mcqs.question: In which scheduling algorithm does the process with the smallest burst time get CPU first?
mcqs.explanation: Shortest Job First (SJF) selects the process with the smallest CPU burst time from the ready queue.

mcqs.question: What is the main disadvantage of FCFS scheduling?

options.text: It does not support preemptive scheduling

mcqs.explanation: FCFS suffers from the convoy effect where short processes wait for long processes to complete, leading to poor average waiting time.

mcqs.question: Round Robin scheduling is best suited for which type of systems?
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin is ideal for time-shared systems as it provides fair CPU allocation and good response time for interactive processes.

mcqs.question: What problem does aging solve in CPU scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Aging gradually increases the priority of waiting processes over time, preventing starvation of low-priority processes.

mcqs.question: In a system with a time quantum of 10ms, if process P1 requires 35ms of CPU time, how many times will it be preempted?
mcqs.explanation: With a 10ms quantum: 1st slice (10ms), 2nd slice (10ms), 3rd slice (10ms), remaining 5ms in 4th slice. It is preempted 3 times.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm gives minimum average waiting time?
mcqs.explanation: SJF (with known burst times) provides the minimum average waiting time among all scheduling algorithms.

mcqs.question: What type of scheduling is Round Robin?
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin is inherently preemptive because processes are forcibly removed from CPU after their time quantum expires.

mcqs.question: In multilevel feedback queue scheduling, a process that uses too much CPU time is moved to:
mcqs.explanation: Processes consuming more CPU time are demoted to lower priority queues to favor interactive processes.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is a preemptive scheduling algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: SRTF (Shortest Remaining Time First) is the preemptive version of SJF that preempts when a shorter process arrives.

mcqs.question: What is the turnaround time of a process?

options.text: Time from CPU arrival to completion
Time spent waiting in ready queue
Time from arrival to first CPU response

mcqs.explanation: Turnaround time = Completion time - Arrival time. It includes waiting time + execution time + I/O time.

mcqs.question: Context switching overhead is highest in which scheduling algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Round Robin has the highest context switching overhead because processes are frequently preempted after each time quantum.

mcqs.question: Starvation can occur in which scheduling algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Priority scheduling can cause starvation where low-priority processes may never get CPU time if higher-priority processes keep arriving.

mcqs.question: If all processes arrive at time 0, which algorithm will have the same order for both waiting time and turnaround time?
mcqs.explanation: When all processes arrive at time 0, SJF and FCFS produce the same ordering of waiting and turnaround times.

mcqs.question: What happens when the time quantum in Round Robin is too large?
mcqs.explanation: If time quantum is very large, Round Robin degenerates to FCFS as processes complete before their quantum expires.

mcqs.question: Which scheduling algorithm is used in real-time systems?
mcqs.explanation: Rate Monotonic Scheduling is a priority-based preemptive algorithm commonly used in real-time systems.

mcqs.question: The ready queue in FCFS is managed as:
mcqs.explanation: FCFS uses a First-In-First-Out (FIFO) queue where processes are served in the order they arrive.

mcqs.question: What is the effect of increasing time quantum on average waiting time in Round Robin?
mcqs.explanation: Very small quantum increases context switching; very large makes it FCFS. There is an optimal quantum that minimizes waiting time.

mcqs.question: In priority scheduling, if two processes have the same priority, which algorithm is used as tie-breaker?
mcqs.explanation: When priorities are equal, FCFS is typically used as the tie-breaker in priority scheduling.

mcqs.question: Multilevel queue scheduling typically separates processes based on:

options.text: Process characteristics or response time requirements

mcqs.explanation: Multilevel queue separates processes based on their characteristics like interactive vs batch, system vs user processes.
